title: The American West
description: This is the West, sir. When the legend becomes fact, print the legend.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did James Marshall discover when he was overseeing the construction of a California sawmill in 1848?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Steel discovery and production did not take place in California.

choices.content: Gold! The discovery set off the Gold Rush of 1849.
An abundance of steel, which would provide enough material to expand railways westward.
An ancient Indian burial site that included a wealth of precious metals.

assessment_items.question_content:
The possibility of great wealth suddenly arose during the **Gold Rush**.  How did this change the racial dynamics of California?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Is it more likely that the possibility of sudden individual wealth would inspire people of different groups to work together, or to compete against each other?

choices.content: Competition over mining claims led to xenophobia and racial violence against immigrants.
New opportunity leveled the playing field for people of all races and ethnic backgrounds, encouraging cooperation.
Chinese and Latin American immigrants banded together with white settlers to force Native Americans off land where they prospected for gold.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the **Homestead Act** guarantee for American citizens?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Homestead Act agreement revolved specifically around working the land, not working in any capacity for the government.

choices.content: A 160-acre plot of land, in exchange for working the land for 5 years.
A free house in exchange for working as a sharecropper.
A 160-acre plot of land, in exchange for working in any capacity for the federal government.

assessment_items.question_content:
What were **Indian reservations** mainly meant to accomplish?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The **main** reason for white Americans instituting Indian reservations was not related to culture.

choices.content: They kept Native Americans off of lands that white Americans wanted to settle.
White Americans gave Native Americans an opportunity to get involved in local government.
They kept Native Americans from assimilating into European American culture.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the 1876 **Battle of the Little Bighorn** go for the U.S. Army?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Colonel Custer was killed in this battle.

choices.content: It was an utter rout; Colonel George Custer was killed and the 7th cavalry was defeated.
It was a great success; Custer and the 7th Cavalry defeated the Sioux army and officially ended the Indian Wars.
It had mixed results; although the 7th Cavalry won their battle with the Sioux army, Colonel George Custer was killed.

assessment_items.question_content:
What consequences did members of the 7th US Cavalry undergo for participating in the massacre at **Wounded Knee**?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Members of the US Army did receive medals for participating in the massacre.

choices.content: Twenty US Army personnel were awarded the Medal of Honor, and the event was generally regarded as favorable by the American public.
The soldiers involved in the massacre were dishonorably discharged from the US Cavalry and 20 of them were sent to jail.
While twenty Army personnel were awarded the Medal of Honor, the white American public looked upon the massacre as a disgrace and an affront to their values.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the prophet Wovoka promise would come about as a result of the **Ghost Dance**?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Part of Wovoka’s prophecy involved the buffalo returning.

choices.content: The buffalo, hunted to near-extinction, would return; white settlers would be banished from Indian territory; and the spirits of the dead would return to aid the living in combat.
The white settlers would all but wipe out the American Indian population, but Indians would be united with their ancestors in the afterlife, where they would build a new world.
The buffalo, hunted to near-extinction, would return from the afterlife and trample the majority of the white settlers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Under the **Dawes Act of 1887**, how would Native Americans be eligible for American citizenship?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The federal government did not require Native Americans to find and settle their own territory.

choices.content: They would have to disavow tribal practices and cultivate a parcel of land in the settler’s fashion.
They would have to register as members of one of the “Five Civilized Tribes:” Cherokee, Chickasaw, Chocktaw, Creek, and Seminole.
They would have to find and cultivate their own land west of the Mississippi River.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the **Dawes Act** challenge Native American authority?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The US Indian Reorganization Act came after the Dawes Act, in 1934 under Franklin Delano Roosevelt’s administration.

choices.content: It attempted to destroy tribal governing councils and confiscate tribal land.
It negated the US Indian Reorganization Act, which had allowed Native Americans to form their own tribal governments.
It forcibly displaced the Five Civilized Tribes form the southeast United States, and put them on a deadly journey towards the plains.
